findings.message: New public endpoint `count_items` (GET /items/count) has no docstring. Other public route handlers in this file/module (e.g. `get_items`, `heartbeat`, `health`, `metrics`) document purpose and contract; this one should too.
findings.suggestion: Add a docstring explaining what /items/count returns and its shape, e.g. """Return the total number of items as {\"count\": int} — companion to GET /items."""

findings.message: New public endpoint GET /items/count is missing from the README's Endpoints section, which currently lists `/items` but not `/items/count`. Public API surface and README are now out of sync.
findings.suggestion: Add a bullet under README.md ‘Endpoints’: `- GET /items/count — total item count` and regenerate the OpenAPI spec (`task openapi:export`) so `openapi.json` reflects the new route (otherwise the contract CI job will fail by design).

findings.message: The new endpoint lacks a `response_model` (unlike `get_items` which uses `response_model=list[ItemRead]`) and returns a bare `dict` annotation. This produces an undocumented/loosely-typed schema in the generated OpenAPI spec.
findings.suggestion: Declare a small response model (e.g. `class ItemCount(BaseModel): count: int`) and set `response_model=ItemCount` so the API contract is explicit in OpenAPI and the docs.
